PANEL OF ARBITRATORS ASSIGNED. REJECTION DATE SEP-26-2013.
MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-30-2013 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT NAN CHEW'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING ANSWERS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND TO COMPEL RESPONSES AND PRODUCTION TO REQEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; REQEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS; REQUEST FOR EXTENSION OF TIME TO ARBITRATE CASE. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL AND FOR SANCTIONS IS UNOPPOSED. THE MOTION IS GRANTED TO THE FOLLOWING EXTENT: THE PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE 20 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF SERVICE OF THIS ORDER TO RESPOND (WITHOUT OBJECTION) TO THE OUTSTANDING DISCOVER; SANCTIONS IN THE SUM OF $1,578.00 ARE AWARDED AND SHALL BE PAID WITHIN THIRTY DAYS FROM THE DATE OF SERVICE OF THIS ORDER. THE COURT IS WITHOUT JURISDICTION TO GRANT THE REQUEST TO EXTEND THE TIME TO COMPLETE ARBITRATION. THAT PORTION OF THE MOTION IS DENIED, WITHOUT PREJUDICE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM STEVEN STEIN, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Plaintiff's counsel is sanctioned $400.00 for failure to appear. The Order to Show Cause is reset to JUN-02-2015 at 10:30 AM in Department 610. JUDGE JOHN K. STEWART; Clerk KCox; Proceedings not reported. (610)

ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-28-2014 Continued To Nov-25-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Compelling Answers To Form Interrogatories And To Compel Responses And Production To Req.'S For Production Of Documents; Req. For Monetary Sanctions; Req. For Extension Of Time To Arbitrate Case
Off Calendar Dec-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances, Atty. David Zucca of Law Office of David Zucca (925-257-2914) No other appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT NAN CHEW's Motion For An Order Compelling Answers To Form Interrogatories And To Compel Responses And Production To Reqest For Production Of Documents; Reqest For Monetary Sanctions; Request For Extension Of Time To Arbitrate Case. Defendant's motion to compel and for sanctions is unopposed. The motion is granted to the following extent: The Plaintiff shall have 20 days from the date of service of this Order to respond (without objection) to the outstanding discover; Sanctions in the sum of $1,578.00 are awarded and shall be paid within thirty days from the date of service of this Order. The Court is without jurisdiction to grant the request to extend the time to complete arbitration. That portion of the motion is denied, without prejudice. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)
